title: achras lobulata
intro: Achras lobulata, also known as the West Indian sapote, is a species of fruit-bearing tree native to Central and South America. The plant's seeds, bark, and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Achras lobulata include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions made from the leaves and seeds, as well as tinctures and extracts used in modern medicine.
